description: Fix remaining cross-platform gaps not covered by the first cross-platform PRD: Windows-style path detection in resolver.ts and config.ts, E2E test chmod calls, and tilde expansion on Windows.

userStories.title: Fix isLocalPath in resolver.ts to recognize Windows paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the isLocalPath check in resolver.ts to recognize Windows-style paths so that Windows users can specify local plugin paths with backslashes or drive letters.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts isLocalPath (line 61) recognizes '.\', '..\', and drive-letter paths like 'C:\' or 'D:/'
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts, lines 61-62. Current code: `const isLocalPath = (specifier: string): boolean => specifier.startsWith('./') || specifier.startsWith('../') || specifier.startsWith('/') || specifier.startsWith('~/');`. Add checks for backslash variants and Windows drive letters. Suggested fix: `const isLocalPath = (specifier: string): boolean => specifier.startsWith('./') || specifier.startsWith('.\\') || specifier.startsWith('../') || specifier.startsWith('..\\') || specifier.startsWith('/') || specifier.startsWith('~/') || /^[A-Za-z]:[/\\]/.test(specifier);`. Note: The parallel PRD (US-003) fixes the path separator in isAllowedPluginPath on line 53 but its notes explicitly say lines 58-62 are 'correct as-is'. That assessment is wrong for Windows users — this story fixes the gap.

userStories.title: Fix isLocalPathEntry in config.ts to recognize Windows paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the config path detection to recognize Windows-style local plugin paths so that config.json entries with backslash paths are treated as local plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts isLocalPathEntry (line 146-147) recognizes '.\', '..\', and drive-letter paths
Same path patterns as US-001 are recognized
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts, lines 146-147. Current code: `const isLocalPathEntry = (s: string): boolean => s.startsWith('./') || s.startsWith('../') || s.startsWith('/') || s.startsWith('~/');`. Apply the exact same fix as US-001. Consider extracting this into a shared helper in @opentabs-dev/shared (or the cross-platform.ts module from the parallel PRD's US-001) to avoid duplication — both resolver.ts and config.ts have the same function. If the shared cross-platform module already exists when this story runs, import from there. If not, duplicate the logic for now and leave a comment noting the duplication.

userStories.title: Fix resolveLocalPath to handle tilde on Windows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tilde expansion in plugin paths to work correctly on Windows where ~ is not a shell convention.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts resolveLocalPath (line 69) correctly expands '~/' on all platforms using os.homedir()
On Windows, '~\' is also recognized and expanded (e.g., '~\plugins\foo')
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts, lines 69-74. Current code only handles '~/' prefix. On Windows, users might type '~\plugins\foo'. Add a check for '~\' prefix: `if (specifier.startsWith('~/') || specifier.startsWith('~\\')) { return resolve(homedir(), specifier.slice(2)); }`. The os.homedir() call is already cross-platform (returns C:\Users\xxx on Windows), so the expansion logic itself is fine — only the prefix detection needs the backslash variant.

userStories.title: Fix E2E fixtures chmod calls for Windows compatibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E test setup to not fail on Windows where chmod is a no-op on NTFS.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/fixtures.ts line 182: fs.chmodSync(configDir, 0o700) is wrapped to handle Windows gracefully
e2e/fixtures.ts line 200: fs.chmodSync(configPath, 0o600) is wrapped to handle Windows gracefully
On Windows, chmod calls are skipped or wrapped in try-catch (NTFS ignores POSIX permissions)
On Unix, chmod behavior is unchanged
userStories.notes: File: e2e/fixtures.ts, lines 182 and 200. These are in the createTestConfigDir function. Current code calls fs.chmodSync directly. On Windows, chmodSync is a partial no-op (Node.js only supports setting the read-only flag via the write bit). The calls won't throw on Windows but won't set real permissions either. Wrap in a platform check: `if (process.platform !== 'win32') { fs.chmodSync(path, mode); }`. This makes the intent clear — on Windows we skip the POSIX permission call. The parallel PRD handles chmod in platform packages via the shared atomicWrite helper, but E2E fixtures are separate (they use Node.js fs, not Bun, since Playwright runs under Node.js).
